RESPONSE TO PETITION TO ALLOW FOR SOCIAL AND ELECTRONIC MEDIA DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 66102828) FILED BY OTHER TWITTER, INC. (NON PARTY)
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-24-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-24-2020 HEARING RE: PETITIONER LAKESIDE SCHOOL, BERNIE NOE'S HEARING ON PETITION FOR ORDER TO ALLOW FOR SOCIAL AND ELECTRONIC MEDIA DISCOVERY, THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: MATTER IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2020 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL TO FILE AND SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF MR. NOE BY DECEMBER 11, 2020. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL TO FILE AND SERVE NOTICE OF THE DECEMBER 15, 2020 HEARING ON ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR NO. 9689, CMANDIA@SFTC.ORG (302/EPS)
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-15-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

PETITION TO ALLOW FOR SOCIAL AND ELECTRONIC MEDIA/ SECOND DECLARATION OF BERNIE NOE (TRANSACTION ID # 200021112) FILED BY PETITIONER LAKESIDE SCHOOL A WASHINGTON PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION NOE, BERNIE AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS LAKESIDE SCHOOL AND BERNIE NOE'S PETITION TO ALLOW FOR SOCIAL AND ELECTRONIC MEDIA DISCOVERY IS GRANTED. UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, AN INTERNET SERVICE PROVIDER OR SOCIAL MEDIA COMPANY SUCH AS TWITTER, INC. MAY BE COMPELLED TO DISCLOSE THE IDENTITY OF AN ANONYMOUS OR PSEUDONYMOUS USER WHEN THE PARTY SEEKING THE INFORMATION SATISFIES TWO REQUIREMENTS: (1) THE PARTY MUST ATTEMPT TO NOTIFY THE ANONYMOUS USER, AND (2) THE PARTY MUST MAKE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT A CASE FOR DEFAMATION EXISTS. (KRINSKY V. DOE 6 (2008) 159 CAL.APP.4TH 1154, 1172; ACCORD, YELP INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2017) 17 CAL.APP.5TH 1, 14.) PLAINTIFFS HAVE SATISFIED BOTH ELEMENTS, AND NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED EITHER BY TWITTER OR BY THE ANONYMOUS USER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600,

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Order To Allow For Social And Electronic Media Discovery
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONERs LAKESIDE SCHOOL, BERNIE NOE's Petition For Order To Allow For Social And Electronic Media Discovery:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Luis A. Barba (949.467.9610; lbarba@CMBG3.COM) of CMBG3 Law for petitioner.
Michael Bleicher (650.838.4490; MBleicher@perkinscoie.com) of Perkins Coie LLP for Twitter.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Lakeside School and Bernie Noe's petition to allow for social and electronic media discovery is granted. Under California law, an internet service provider or social media company such as Twitter, Inc. may be compelled to disclose the identity of an anonymous or pseudonymous user when the party seeking the information satisfies two requirements: (1) the party must attempt to notify the anonymous user, and (2) the party must make a prima facie showing that a case for defamation exists. (Krinsky v. Doe 6 (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1154, 1172; accord, Yelp Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 1, 14.) Plaintiffs have satisfied both elements, and no opposition has been filed either by Twitter or by the anonymous user.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

HEARING RE: PETITIONER LAKESIDE SCHOOL, BERNIE NOE's Hearing On Petition For Order To Allow For Social And Electronic Media Discovery.
